seoTitle: How to Add a Follow-Up Reminder to a Sent Email
metaDescription: Learn how to add a follow-up reminder to an Outlook email, either before sending or after, and how to flag reminders for your recipient too.
intro: Chasing down important client deliverables shouldn't rely on sticky notes or memory alone. Outlook's follow-up flag system lets you attach a reminder to an email before you send it, or add one after the fact from your Sent Items folder. This guide covers both methods plus flagging for the recipient.

steps.heading: Flag before sending
steps.body: Open a new email, click into the body, then go to the Message tab and open the Follow Up dropdown, then select Add Reminder.

steps.heading: Set the reminder details
steps.body: Check Flag for Me, pick a start date, a due date, and check the Reminder alert box.

steps.heading: Flag for the recipient too
steps.body: Check the Flag for Recipients option if you want a matching tracking prompt to appear on their screen as well.

steps.heading: Send the message
steps.body: Click OK to lock the reminder, finish your message, and click Send.

steps.heading: Add a reminder after sending
steps.body: Open your Sent Items folder, find the email, right-click the small flag icon on the message row, and select Add Reminder.

steps.heading: Check your task list
steps.body: Open the To-Do or Task panel in the lower application tray to confirm your reminder appears among your active items.

faq.question: Can I add a reminder to an email I already sent?
faq.answer: Yes, open the message in your Sent Items folder and use the flag icon on the message row to add a reminder retroactively.

faq.question: Does flagging for the recipient force them to see a reminder?
faq.answer: It adds a tracking prompt to their copy of the message, though how prominently it displays depends on their own mail client.

faq.question: Where do my follow-up reminders show up?
faq.answer: They appear in the centralized To-Do or Task panel in the lower application tray.

faq.question: Can I set both a start date and a due date?
faq.answer: Yes, the reminder dialog lets you specify a start date and a separate due date along with an alert.
